params.name: Crookes Tube
params.description:
On Jun 19, 1875 AD, the British physicist, Duke Crookes, developed the cathode-ray tube.
A Crookes tube, also known as a cathode-ray tube, developed by British physicist Crookes, is an electron beam tube that can convert electrical signals into optical images. It mainly consists of an electron gun, a deflection system, a tube shell, and a fluorescent screen. It can emit an electron beam, which is displayed in the form of photoelectricity on the fluorescent screen, and the deflection of the el …
